payload.answer: Rejected. Do not store an entire transcript as a conversation_summary memory card. Memory should be concise, durable, and atomic; transcripts should be summarized into specific reusable facts only when they are clearly supported and likely to remain useful.

entity_resolution.reason: The proposal does not identify durable, atomic user facts or stable preferences. A full transcript is not appropriate to commit as memory.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether an LLM proposal to store an entire 8000-word transcript as one conversation_summary memory card should be accepted.

details: Blocked proposed memory because it attempts to store an entire 8000-word transcript as one card.
No durable memory cards were accepted.

repair_options: Extract only durable user preferences, biographical facts, commitments, or stable project context from the transcript.
Split any accepted information into atomic memory cards rather than one large card.
Preserve uncertainty and attribution when the transcript is ambiguous.
Do not commit ephemeral conversation details, raw dialogue, or superseded/deleted information as current memory.
